activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.

activities.title: Geometric Models for Identities
activities.details: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models. Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates. Compute the distance between two points.

activities.title: Euclid's Definitions and Constructions
activities.details: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras. Justify simple constructions using axioms.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation Activities
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape. Explain how ancient civilisations approximated the area of a circle.

activities.title: Data Analysis Projects
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question. Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.

projects.title: Portfolio Assessment
projects.details: Internal Assessment: Portfolio (05 Marks). Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling Projects
projects.details: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations (graphs, tables, equations) to draw conclusions. Models real-life situations in the form of equations.

practicals.title: Lab Practical (Mathematics Laboratory)
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books. Prescribed Books: Mathematics - Textbook for class IX, Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX, Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage.
